Service providers have the ability to send instructions to specified topics via MQTT. When our system receives data from a specific topic, it will automatically execute the corresponding functions based on the pre - configured interfaces. For example, service providers can send instructions to adjust device operation parameters through a specific topic. After our system receives such an instruction, it will accurately call the corresponding interface to complete the adjustment of device parameters.
It should be noted that during the data transmission process, the size limit for a single piece of data is 256KB. This specification ensures the stable and efficient transmission of data across different devices and network environments, avoiding transmission errors or performance issues caused by overly large data. Strictly adhering to the MQTT data size standard helps maintain the stability and reliability of the entire message transmission system and ensures the smooth progress of the instruction sending and execution process.
